title: The Little Penguin Who Made a Mistake
moral: Being honest takes real courage. When we tell the truth about our mistakes, we become braver and our friends trust us even more.
moralZh: 承认错误需要真正的勇气。当我们诚实地面对自己的过错时，我们会变得更勇敢，朋友也会更加信任我们。

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: It was a sunny morning in Penguin Village. Pip the little penguin kicked his blue ball high into the air.

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Watch this, Penny! I am going to kick it all the way to the big rock!"

paragraphs.speaker: Penny
paragraphs.text: "Be careful, Pip! Mrs. Bear's house is right behind that rock!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But Pip kicked the ball too hard. It flew past the rock and — CRASH! — went straight through Mrs. Bear's window!

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Oh no! I broke Mrs. Bear's window! She is going to be so angry with me!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pip quickly picked up his ball and ran home. He hid it under his bed and pretended nothing had happened.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But Pip did not feel happy. He sat on his bed with his head down. His tummy felt all twisty and worried.

paragraphs.speaker: Penny
paragraphs.text: "Pip, what is wrong? You look very sad today."

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "I... I broke Mrs. Bear's window with my ball. I am scared she will be very angry with me."

paragraphs.speaker: Penny
paragraphs.text: "Oh, Pip. Making a mistake is not the worst thing. The worst thing is not telling the truth. You need to be honest."

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "But what if she is really, really angry?"

paragraphs.speaker: Penny
paragraphs.text: "Being brave means doing the right thing even when you are scared. You should go tell her the truth."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pip took a deep breath. His tummy felt twisty, but he knew Penny was right.

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Okay. I will go tell Mrs. Bear. Will you come with me?"

paragraphs.speaker: Penny
paragraphs.text: "Of course I will. You do not have to be scared. I am right here with you."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The two little penguins walked together to Mrs. Bear's house. Pip's heart went boom-boom-boom very fast.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pip knocked on the door. Mrs. Bear opened it with a surprised face.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Bear
paragraphs.text: "Oh! Little Pip, what can I do for you today?"

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Mrs. Bear... I... I broke your window. It was my ball. I am very sorry. Please forgive me."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Mrs. Bear looked at the broken window. Then she looked at Pip's scared face.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Bear
paragraphs.text: "Oh my! My window! Oh dear... But Pip, I am not angry. I am proud of you for telling the truth. That took real courage."

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "You... you are not angry?"

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Bear
paragraphs.text: "No, dear. You made a mistake — it was an accident. But you were brave and honest. That is worth more than any window."

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "I promise I will be more careful next time. Can we help fix your window?"

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Bear
paragraphs.text: "That is a wonderful idea! Let us go ask Leo the Lion. He has all the right tools to fix a window."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pip, Penny, and Mrs. Bear went to Leo's house. Leo the Lion loved helping his friends.

paragraphs.speaker: Leo
paragraphs.text: "A broken window? No problem! I have all the tools we need. Let us all work together!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The next day, all the animals worked together to fix the window. Leo held the new glass. Pip and Penny passed him tools. Mrs. Bear made fresh lemonade.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Bear
paragraphs.text: "Look! The window is as good as new! And look — I put your flowers back in their place."

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Thank you, Mrs. Bear. I was so scared, but I am glad I told the truth. My tummy feels much better now."

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Bear
paragraphs.text: "You were very brave today, Pip. Honest people are the best friends to have. I trust you even more now."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pip learned something very important that day. Making a mistake is okay — everyone makes mistakes. But being honest about them takes real courage. And when you tell the truth, your friends will trust you even more than before.
